clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Prevent or treat elevated BP and hypertension through physical activity
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adults with or without hypertension during routine care, preventive health visits, or hypertension management
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Prescribe structured exercise program (aerobic + resistance training) for BP management

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for physical activity recommendation
Assess current activity level and contraindications
Set weekly activity goals with patient

validationRules: Baseline BP if available for tracking

rules: Apply recommendation to all adults with or without hypertension
Prescribe structured exercise program including both aerobic exercise AND resistance training
Goal: prevent elevated BP in normotensive adults
Goal: treat hypertension in adults with diagnosed hypertension
Monitor BP response to exercise over time

categories.name: Physical Activity Recommendation
categories.outcome: Prevent or treat elevated BP and hypertension

recommendation.description: In adults with or without hypertension, increasing physical activity, through a structured exercise program that includes aerobic exercise and/or resistance training, is recommended to prevent or treat elevated BP and hypertension.

suggestedPhrasing.description: Is recommended / Is indicated / Should be performed

appDisplay.behavior: Proceed to goal setting and follow-up scheduling

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Prescribe structured exercise program - refer to cardiac rehab or exercise physiologist if available
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Reassess BP in 4-12 weeks after starting exercise program
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor BP before and after exercise sessions initially
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag if patient has contraindications or develops symptoms
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log exercise prescription and baseline BP to health record
